title: Static Frontend Hosting on Free/Low-Cost Platforms
description: Host static frontends using free/low-cost static hosting platforms (e.g., Netlify/Vercel/Cloudflare Pages) to minimize hosting OPEX; validate quotas, SLAs, and compliance needs.

cost.costNotes: Estimates are intentionally conservative; verify against current pricing before committing. Free tiers are quota-limited; production use may require paid plans and compliance agreements.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min ($0) represents free tier usage for low-traffic sites (e.g., Netlify free: 100GB bandwidth, Vercel free: 100GB bandwidth, Cloudflare Pages unlimited requests on free tier). Max ($200) represents mid-tier paid plans needed when exceeding free quotas - typically 1TB+ bandwidth, custom domains, team collaboration features, and SLA guarantees. Production sites with moderate traffic (500K-1M monthly visitors) typically fall in $20-200/month range. High-traffic enterprise scenarios would exceed this range but aren't the primary use case for this cost-optimization pattern.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium complexity adoption. Requires: (1) Frontend build pipeline integration (2-4 hours: configuring CI/CD, build commands, environment variables), (2) DNS configuration and SSL setup (1-2 hours), (3) Migration of existing assets if applicable (2-8 hours depending on current hosting), (4) Testing deployment workflow and rollback procedures (2-4 hours), (5) Team training on platform-specific features like preview deployments, edge functions (2-4 hours). Total 10-20 engineer hours at $50-75/hour blended rate. Low learning curve for teams familiar with Git-based deployments, but requires understanding of platform limitations, quota monitoring, and fallback strategies for compliance requirements.
provenance.licenseCost: All major static hosting platforms (Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, AWS Amplify, Azure Static Web Apps, GCP Firebase Hosting) offer free tiers with no upfront license costs. These are usage-based SaaS platforms - costs scale with consumption (bandwidth, builds, team seats) rather than licensing. No proprietary tooling or software licenses required; uses standard web technologies and open-source build tools (webpack, Vite, Next.js, etc.).

provides.reasoning: This pattern specifically provides static file hosting for frontend applications, which is its primary purpose. The capability name is clear and specific.
Free/low-cost platforms like Netlify, Vercel, and Cloudflare Pages include global CDN distribution as a core feature, making this a valid provided capability for content delivery optimization.
These platforms include free SSL certificates by default and handle HTTPS termination automatically for all hosted sites.
These platforms offer built-in deployment pipelines that automatically deploy on git push or merge, eliminating manual deployment steps.
Most of these platforms support branch/PR previews, automatically creating temporary preview URLs for each pull request or branch.
These platforms handle DNS configuration and custom domain setup, though the actual domain registration typically happens externally.

requires.reasoning: Static hosting platforms require pre-built static assets (HTML, CSS, JS) to deploy. The pattern depends on a build process that produces these artifacts.
Platforms like Netlify, Vercel, and Cloudflare Pages typically integrate with Git repositories for automated deployments, making source control a practical requirement.

supports_nfr.reason: Free/low-cost static hosting platforms lack SLA guarantees; availability targets above 99.5% may not be reliably met without enterprise-grade hosting.
CDN-backed static hosting achieves p95 latency of at least 50ms including edge propagation.
CDN cache misses and regional variations result in p99 latency of at least 200ms.
Static hosting platforms provide instant rollback to previous deployments achieving RTO of at least 1 minute.
Free/low-cost tiers enforce bandwidth and request quotas limiting throughput to 100M jobs/hour.
Free/low-cost static hosting platforms have bandwidth and request quotas that may be exceeded at high traffic, risking service throttling or unexpected costs.
Static hosting write operations (deployments) are infrequent and rate-limited by CI/CD pipelines.
Free/low-cost static hosting platforms may not provide compliance certifications (GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2) required for PII handling.
Pattern is GDPR-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is CCPA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is HIPAA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling and provider BAAs, not this pattern.
Pattern is SOX-agnostic; compliance depends on audit controls in the application layer, not this pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Static hosting platforms like Netlify/Vercel/Cloudflare Pages are cloud-agnostic and multi-cloud compatible.
Static hosting serves pre-built HTML/CSS/JS assets regardless of the build language or framework used.
Static hosting platforms specifically serve web frontends via HTTP/HTTPS.

provider.description: Static hosting provider choice. Netlify: generous free tier, simple setup. Vercel: optimized for Next.js/React, edge functions. Cloudflare Pages: global CDN, Workers integration, unlimited bandwidth on free tier

cdn_enabled.description: Enable CDN edge caching for global distribution. Reduces latency and bandwidth costs but may complicate cache invalidation

auto_ssl.description: Automatic SSL/TLS certificate provisioning and renewal. Essential for production but may require DNS configuration

build_command_required.description: Whether platform should run build commands (npm run build) or deploy pre-built artifacts. Pre-built reduces build minutes usage on free tiers

preview_deployments.description: Enable automatic preview deployments for pull requests. Useful for testing but consumes quota on free tiers
